findings.evidence: FunctorOfPoints.lean:165-167 marks only mapValue_apply @[simp] with no pointwise simp twin; WithConv.lean:42 shows ofConv_toconv is not @[simp]; WithConv.lean:60 CoeFun has no reduction simp lemma.
findings.fix: Mirror the mapValue convention: keep only the WithConv-level mapDomain_apply as @[simp] and demote mapDomain_apply_apply (and mapDomain_inv_apply) to plain lemmas; or, if a pointwise normal form is wanted, drop @[simp] from mapDomain_apply instead so the pointwise lemmas become the normal form.
findings.issue: mapDomain_apply (line 62) and mapDomain_apply_apply (line 67) are both @[simp]. simp rewrites the subterm `mapDomain φ f` via mapDomain_apply first, producing `⇑(toConv (f.ofConv.comp φ)) h`; since ofConv_toConv is not @[simp] (WithConv.lean:42) and no coe-of-toConv simp lemma exists, the term is stuck there and mapDomain_apply_apply can never match. The intended pointwise normal form `f.ofConv (φ h)` is not reached, and mapDomain_apply_apply is a dead simp lemma.

findings.evidence: Lemma LHS `mapDomain (...) φ (f⁻¹) h` (line 121-123) is headed by `mapDomain`, which mapDomain_apply (line 62, @[simp]) rewrites away in post-order subterm simplification.
findings.fix: Drop @[simp] (keep it as the explicit pointwise formula), or resolve the mapDomain_apply/mapDomain_apply_apply simp conflict above so the inverse form can be reached.
findings.issue: mapDomain_inv_apply is @[simp] but is shadowed by the same mechanism: on `mapDomain φ f⁻¹ h`, mapDomain_apply rewrites the `mapDomain φ f⁻¹` subterm first, so this lemma's LHS never matches and the antipode form it records is never produced by simp.

summary: Surface is minimal, bodies hidden, and the characteristic API is complete and well-parallel to mapValue. But the pointwise @[simp] lemmas are shadowed by the WithConv-level mapDomain_apply @[simp], so they never fire and pointwise terms are left un-normalized.
